description: Gercek stripped binary baseline olcumu. DIKKAT: Bu olcumlerin buyuk kismi METODOLOJIK SORUNLARI ortaya cikardi; gorunen F1/precision degerleri sahte-yuksek. Detaylar 'analysis' bolumunde.

sample_macho_debug.notes: v1.11.0 baseline. Debug symbols present, misleading.

sample_macho_stripped.strip_level: strip (default) - BUT export table still intact
sample_macho_stripped.notes: TRIVIAL: nm GT has only __mh_execute_header. Karadul did not rename anything new. F1=1/1 is an artifact of GT size.

libsqlite3_stripped.analyze_status: TIMEOUT at 15min (static=763s done, deobfuscate=5.6s done, reconstruct partial, report incomplete) - naming_map.json was produced before timeout
libsqlite3_stripped.notes: TRIVIAL-HIGH: All 348 GT symbols matched via symbols.json cross-ref (export table preserved in dylib). Per-symbol 'recovered' is literally the nm name. Karadul's real contribution (naming 2696-348=2348 internal functions) is NOT measured here.

methodological_issues: Issue 1: macOS 'strip' (default) does not remove the export symbol table from Mach-O exe/dylib. The test fixtures used here are NOT truly stripped in the 'every function is FUN_<addr>' sense.

Issue 2: benchmark_runner.find_symbol_mapping reads static/symbols.json (which contains nm output) and uses it to cross-reference ground truth back to preserved names. When nm symbols survive, GT lookups trivially succeed even if karadul naming_map is empty.

Issue 3: Ground truth is generated via nm, which only sees export symbols. Non-export internal functions (the ones karadul actually renames) are never in GT, so karadul's real naming quality is untested.

Issue 4: FUN_ residue is computed over flat(naming_map). karadul's per_function keys never start with FUN_ (they get the Ghidra symbol-table name which retained export/thunk names), so residue is near-0 regardless of quality.

debug_vs_stripped_delta.interpretation: Delta is nonsensical: debug build had 6 GT symbols and karadul mis-named 1 of them, stripped build has 1 GT symbol and got it exact. Both measurements are fragile.

real_residue_per_fixture.sample_macho_stripped: 0/6 FUN_ (0%) in ghidra_functions.json - all names came from export table
real_residue_per_fixture.libsqlite3_stripped: 4/2696 FUN_ (0.15%) in ghidra_functions.json - 99.85% names preserved by Ghidra/export

recommendations.ci_gate: DO NOT switch CI gate to these 'stripped' fixtures yet. They measure trivial preservation, not naming quality. Current sample_macho baseline is misleading but at least exercises the rename pipeline (1 wrong rename in 6).
required_fixture_work: Build a TRULY stripped fixture: compile sample_macho with -s and also rewrite Mach-O LC_SYMTAB to drop export names (e.g., use 'strip -Sx' + custom postprocessor, or target ELF with 'strip --strip-all').

Add a Linux ELF fixture (e.g., compile sample.c on Linux or cross-compile, then 'strip --strip-all'). ELF strip removes .symtab cleanly and forces Ghidra to produce FUN_<addr> names. This gives a realistic 100%-FUN_ baseline.
Target metric on truly-stripped fixture: 'how many FUN_<addr> did karadul successfully rename to something meaningful?' rather than 'did it match nm output?'.
benchmark_runner_fix: Remove or gate step 4 of _compare_maps (the 'preserved name via symbols.json' path). Currently this inflates F1 for any binary with intact export tables.
Add 'renamed_from_placeholder' counter: number of GT entries where recovered != placeholder AND recovered != nm-preserved name. This is the true signal.
Split metrics by 'preserved' vs 'recovered': export-symbol GT entries should get their own category and should NOT count toward the main F1.
week2_priority: P0: Add a Linux ELF truly-stripped fixture to the repo (e.g., tests/fixtures/sample_elf_stripped). This unblocks all real metric work.
P1: Fix _compare_maps so 'preserved' and 'karadul-renamed' are distinguished. Update baseline_metrics.json with both numbers.
P2: FUN_ residue must be measured against ghidra_functions.json (full function list), not only flat(naming_map).
P3: Re-compute all deltas with the two fixes above. Current debug -> stripped delta of +0.091 is noise.
recommendations.v1_20_5_target_revision: Keep revising AFTER P0-P1 are done. Any performance target based on current F1=0.909 / F1=1.000 numbers is built on sand.
